item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An impressive twist at the end of a story that, while not hard to follow, really does throw the reader around a couple times.

+1.

item.title: Re: Typo Edit
item.content:encoded:
Glad you liked it, and thanks for the catch!

Listpages are a nightmare to edit - each page is actually a distinct thing going under the category "fragments:." 2/10 would not do again, but I did fix the edit (and also an incorrect page title, while I was at it).

item.title: Typo Edit
item.content:encoded:
Hey, before I get into my edit suggestion, just wanted to say that I appreciate the idea of the SCP Foundation's leniency with its methodology actually leads to repercussions which threaten the normalcy they're supposed to protect. Although I acknowledge that the O-5 council's usual depiction as cool-headed executives is something I normally prefer to the shortsightedness and relative impulsiveness they display here, the circumstances of this situation create an entertaining and realistic (in reference to the O-5's attempt to prevent an overwhelming threat make them temporarily forgo precautions which were created as a safety net) narrative. +1.

Okay, onto the edit: in the article iteration which resembles a standard SCP form, the paragraph which includes the following quote includes a typo in the form of "trauama."
I would edit myself, but I can't access the %%content%% section of text in the article.

Autopsies performed on the bodies of SCP-4884-B late in the process of performing SCP-4884-A instances indicate significant trauama [sic] to the prefrontal cortex, temporal lobe, and pineal gland, originating within and moving outward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The title reminds me of that one Twilight Zone episode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a coincidence - I don't keep up with Sarkicism stuff at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you write this with Sarkicism in mind, or are the similarities a complete accident? Either way, I like this. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wondering what the title meant until I got to the last document.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a sort of saying usually find myself using when reading particularly long entries: Writing something long is fine, but its the author's duty to ensure the ride is entertaining throughout the duration.

Needless to say, this article did just that. From the intro from the O5, to the historical documentation, to the madness spiral of Prometheus Labs (which honestly was one of my favorite parts), to that twist ending, this was quite a thrill ride and I am glad you posted it for the contest.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the author of this page is so fucking handsome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like and dislike many 4k entries, but man, I want THIS to win. Holy shit, this was more terrifying and personal than you might have thought it'd be at the beggining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great article. Didn't see the end coming at all. You do interchange "phage" and "vore" several times on that final page however. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, it's comfortably in the positives now. Guess it's clear it was really of a personal thing to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great article! I like the way it builds, and the twist at the end was good. There's nothing wrong with the way you've set up the listpages; I really don't see how it could break immersion. Overall, good job! I would be happy if this entry won.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it be less immersion-breaking if the addenda were all in one place? The SH document will be separate for reasons of format and style, but everything else could potentially be together. I thought that it would be easier to read with listpages, as it breaks up different sections, but if it's a stumbling block for people, I can change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I thought this was pretty great. :C Disappointed to see it in the negatives so quickly. Here's an injection of positivity moving forward!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The listpage did the exact opposite to me, it broke the immersion completely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A series of documents read by Director Forrester from their console. The listpage is 1) to (hopefully) increase immersion 2) to keep the document at the end from screwing up all of the footnotes, which is what kept happening when I had it in tabs. To quote Ellias Bouchard (?) "You feed it and it feeds you."

The alternate title was "To Serve Great Man," but chose not to go with it. To serve great man! It's a cookbook! Original title was "Artificial Apotheosis," but changed it because that was boring.

Thanks to DrBleep, Tl333s, rumetzen, Vezaz, vivax, ihp, gumbal1, TheGreatHippo

e: Photo is from Public Domain Review. As the name implies, it's public domain.
